description: The short blunted bill was typical of a vireo, not like the sharp pointed bill of a warbler. The size of the bird was about the size of a typical warbler but the bird was chunkier in body. The head was slate gray with white "spectacles" around the eye that met in the front over the bill. The back was olive-gray. The tail was blackish with what looked like paler edges to the outer feathers. The wings were blackish with 2 yellowish-white wing bars and the tertials and secondaries had yellowish edging. The underparts were white at the throat (contrasting with the slate gray face). the white extended down the breast and the center of the belly. The flanks were yellow. The vent area and undertail coverts were yellow. The slate gray crown and nape contrasted noticeable with the olive-gray back. The bird would work slowly along the dead branches foraging for bugs. Dave Leatherman has photos of the bird.
photographer: Photographer: David Leatherman, David Leatherman, David Leatherman, David Leatherman, David Leatherman
